title: IMPACT by Amir Shpilman
summary: Engraving and computer-assisted orchestration for a new opera. Developed a custom Sibelius plugin for real-time material orchestration.

contentBlocks.value: **IMPACT by Amir Shpilman** represents a significant project in the opera genre. The Opera is for orchestra, choir, and two stuntwomen. Our role involved engraving of the score. However, under tight deadlines, pressure, and considering the complexity of some parts of the piece, we had to develop innovative solutions to engrave these sections. These solutions also facilitate the easy generation of performance documentation, reduce rehearsal time, and bring clarity to the performance of the piece through the development of **computer-assisted engraving techniques**.

contentBlocks.alt: Scene from IMPACT opera showing complex staging

contentBlocks.value: A key challenge was the rapid generation and adaptation of musical material. To address this, we developed a **custom Sibelius plugin**. This tool allowed for the systematic notation and orchestration of material in real-time, which was crucial for streamlining the production process and meeting tight deadlines. The plugin integrated directly with the composer's workflow, providing a powerful assistant for complex orchestrational tasks. The visual complexity of the score required precise digital tools, and the plugin helped manage this effectively, allowing for quick iterations and adjustments based on rehearsal feedback. This technological integration was vital to the project's success, enabling a fluid collaboration between the composer, performers, and the technical team. The stuntwomen's actions were also intricately timed with the music, adding another layer of complexity that the engraving and orchestration tools helped to manage.

contentBlocks.alt: Close-up of the custom Sibelius plugin interface

contentBlocks.value: Further details about the performance and its reception can be found on the [official project page](https://www.cultopia.gr/projects/Impact).

title: Kriat Shema by Amir Shpilman (2025)
summary: Score, parts, and performance materials for Amir Shpilman's 'Kriat Shema,' a Fantasy for Shofar and Orchestra. Premiered by the Berlin Philharmonic.

contentBlocks.value:
**ZSCORE.art studio**, under **Parham Behzad**, was responsible for the preparation of the score, individual parts, and all performance materials for Amir Shpilman's new orchestral piece, *'Kriat Shema – Fantasy for Shofar and Orchestra'*. Is the second work we did for him and it was premiered by **Berlin Philharmonic**.

The piece is dedicated to Bar Zemach and Yehezkel Tchorz, RIP.

contentBlocks.alt: Detail from the score of Kriat Shema
contentBlocks.caption: Excerpt from Amir Shpilman's 'Kriat Shema'

contentBlocks.value:
The engraving and material preparation ensured clarity and precision for the orchestral textures and the unique inclusion of the Shofar.

Copyright for the work is held by Amir Shpilman, and all right is resereved. The score typesetting and engraving credited to **Parham Behzad (ZSCORE)**.

title: Engraving for Mass Choir Collaborative Performance
summary: Current, large-scale engraving project for a collaborative performance piece involving a mass choir of over 100 singers.

contentBlocks.value:
This ongoing project involves the detailed engraving of a new work designed for a **mass choir with more than 100 singers**. The focus is on creating clear, accurate, and performer-friendly scores and parts to facilitate a large-scale collaborative performance.

The nature of the work demands careful attention to layout and part extraction to manage the complexities of a large ensemble.

contentBlocks.alt: Example of choral engraving style for the project
contentBlocks.caption: Score preparation for over 100 singers
